meta.title: Quenderin — An AI that runs on your phone, even offline
meta.description: Quenderin runs large language models fully on your device. No internet, no API keys, no telemetry. Download a model once and your assistant works on a plane, in a tent, off the grid.
lang.label: Language
hero.title: An AI that lives on your phone—not in someone’s cloud.
hero.lede: Quenderin runs large language models fully on your device. No internet, no API keys, no telemetry. Download a model once over Wi-Fi, and your assistant keeps working on a plane, in a tent, anywhere your signal doesn’t.
hero.dev.q: Best knot for a tarp shelter?
hero.dev.ai: A taut-line hitch on the ridgeline—it grips under load but slides to re-tension when the wind picks up. For the corners, a prusik loop lets you adjust without untying.
hero.dev.foot: 0 ms to a server · nothing left your phone
trust.label: Local inference, done right
problem.title: The cloud isn’t always there. Your AI shouldn’t vanish with your signal.
problem.body: Every other assistant is a thin client for a data center. Lose connection and it goes dark—and even when it works, your words travel to a server you don’t own. Quenderin flips that: the model lives on the device in your hand.
how.title: Download once. Run forever.
how.s1b: Quenderin reads your hardware—RAM, chip, GPU—and recommends the largest model your device can run well.
how.s2b: Pull the model once over Wi-Fi—0.4 to 4.7 GB, your choice. It resumes in the background if you get interrupted.
how.s3b: Everything runs locally from then on. A clear check tells you it’s safe before you leave the grid.
feat.title: A real assistant, with nothing phoning home.
feat.offline.b: After the one-time download, there are zero network calls. Works at 35,000 feet or three days into a hike.
feat.free.b: No subscriptions, no per-token billing. It runs on hardware you already own.
feat.agent.b: A tool-using agent with a hard safety blocklist—it will never autonomously touch:
feat.priv.b: No accounts, no analytics, no telemetry. Your conversations are yours.
feat.hw.b: It picks a model that fits—and warns you before one that doesn’t.
feat.os.b: MIT licensed. Read every line, audit every claim, build it yourself.
feat.resume.b: The model keeps downloading in the background—even if you switch apps the night before a trip.
story.eyebrow: Built for the edge of the map
story.title: You’re going off the grid for three days.
story.body: The night before, on hotel Wi-Fi, you download a model—and Quenderin keeps the download alive even when you switch apps, then shows a green “ready to go offline”. On the trail, with no bars, you still have a capable assistant in your pocket. That’s the whole point: an AI that doesn’t need the world to be online.
priv.title: Your data never had to leave. So it doesn’t.
priv.c1: No cloud calls after the model is downloaded
priv.c2: No API keys, no sign-in, no account
priv.c3: No analytics, no tracking, no telemetry
priv.c4: Conversations stored only on your device
priv.c5: Open source — verify all of the above yourself
priv.net.foot: 0 B sent since launch · nothing left your device
models.title: Bring any model. Right-sized for your hardware.
models.intro: Quenderin runs on llama.cpp—so it runs any GGUF model: Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Gemma, Phi. Here’s the curated shortlist it recommends from, sized for everything from a Raspberry Pi to an M-series Mac.
models.r8: Best quality for a strong device
models.foot: + thousands more from Hugging Face—any GGUF works. Choosing is optional: Quenderin picks the best fit for your device automatically.
faq.title: Questions, answered.
faq.intro: It mostly comes down to one idea: the model runs on your device, so nothing leaves it. Here’s the rest.
faq.a1: Yes. Quenderin is MIT-licensed open source, and inference runs on your own hardware—so there are no token costs and no subscription.
faq.a2: No. After the one-time model download, Quenderin makes no network calls. There are no analytics, no accounts, and no telemetry. Because it’s open source, you can verify this yourself.
faq.q3: What devices does it run on?
faq.a3: The desktop app runs today on macOS and Linux. Native iOS and Android apps are in active development—the same hardware-detection and model-selection engine, rebuilt natively for performance.
faq.q4: Do I need a powerful phone?
faq.a4: No. Quenderin scales from a Raspberry Pi to an M-series Mac. It detects what your device can handle and recommends a model that actually runs—down to a 0.4 GB ultra-light build.
faq.a5: It’s open source and runnable from GitHub today. The polished mobile apps are on the way—star or watch the repo on GitHub to hear the moment they land.
cta.title: Bring your AI offline.
cta.body: It’s open source and runs from GitHub today. Star the repo to follow along—and watch releases to hear the moment the mobile apps land.
cta.consent: No email, no account, no trackers—just the code. See our Privacy Policy.
foot.note: No cookies, analytics, or trackers—nothing loads from a third party.
hw.lede: Quenderin turns your phone or laptop into a self-contained AI. There's no server doing the thinking for you — the model itself lives on your device. Setup is three steps, and it only happens once.
hw.s1b: On first launch, Quenderin reads what your device actually has — total and free RAM, the chip, and whether there's a usable GPU. It uses that to recommend the largest model your device can run well, not just the largest one that fits. If nothing fits comfortably, it tells you plainly instead of stalling.
hw.s2b: Pick the recommendation or choose your own — anywhere from a 0.4 GB ultra-light build to a 9 GB flagship. The download runs over Wi-Fi and resumes in the background if you switch apps or lose signal, so a big model finishing overnight is no problem. Every download is integrity-checked before it's ever loaded.
hw.s3b: From then on, everything runs locally. Before you leave the grid, a clear check confirms the model is present and ready — a green "ready to go offline". After that, airplane mode, a tunnel, or three days on a trail make no difference.
hw.od.b: Most assistants are a thin app in front of someone else's data center. Quenderin is the opposite: the weights, the inference, and your conversation history all stay on the hardware in your hand.
hw.od.c1: No prompt or response is ever sent to a server
hw.od.c2: No account, no sign-in, no API keys to manage
hw.od.c3: No analytics or telemetry — there's nothing phoning home to disable
hw.od.c4: Conversations are stored locally and are yours to delete
hw.od.c5: The one and only network call is the initial model download
hw.rs.t: It picks a model that actually runs.
hw.rs.b1: Bigger isn't always better on a phone. Quenderin matches the model to your device's memory headroom, leaving room for the operating system so things stay responsive — and it warns you before a model that would swap, stutter, or fail to load.
hw.rs.b2: The result: a capable assistant that feels instant, instead of a flagship model that crawls. You can always override the recommendation and choose your own trade-off.
ft.lede: Quenderin isn't a stripped-down toy because it runs locally — it's a capable assistant that happens to need nobody's servers. Here's what that buys you.
ft.offline.b: After the one-time model download, there are zero network calls. It answers at 35,000 feet, on a train through a tunnel, or three days into a hike with no bars. Offline isn't a degraded mode — it's the normal mode.
ft.free.b: No subscriptions, no per-token billing. Inference runs on hardware you already own.
ft.agent.b: Quenderin includes a tool-using agent that can carry out small tasks — but it ships with a hard safety blocklist it can never override on its own. It will not autonomously pay, delete, transfer money, or touch credentials. Sensitive actions are surfaced for you to confirm, never taken silently.
ft.priv.b: No accounts, no analytics, no telemetry. Your conversations stay on your device — and because it's open source, that's verifiable, not a promise.
ft.hw.b: It picks a model that fits your memory and chip — and warns you before one that doesn't, so it never stutters or fails to load.
ft.os.b: MIT licensed. Read every line, audit every privacy claim, and build it yourself from source.
ft.resume.b: Models keep downloading in the background and resume after an interruption — switch apps the night before a trip and it's still ready by morning.
ft.cta.b: Every feature above works with any model you choose — from a Raspberry Pi build to an M-series flagship.
md.intro: Quenderin runs on llama.cpp, so it runs any GGUF model — Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Gemma, Phi, and thousands more from Hugging Face. Below is the curated shortlist it recommends from, sized for everything from a Raspberry Pi to an M-series Mac.
md.foot: + thousands more from Hugging Face — any GGUF works.
md.rec.b1: Choosing a model is optional. Quenderin detects your device's memory and chip and automatically selects the best fit — leaving headroom for the operating system so the app stays responsive. It steers you away from a model that would swap to disk or fail to load.
md.rec.b2: Prefer to drive? Override the recommendation and load any GGUF you like, including ones you've downloaded yourself.
md.q.b1: The catalog defaults to Q4_K_M quantization — a 4-bit format that cuts a model's size to roughly a quarter of full precision while keeping almost all of its quality. That's what makes a 7-billion-parameter model fit, and run, on a phone.
md.q.b2: Larger quants (more quality, more memory) and smaller ones (lighter, faster) are available for any model you bring.
md.cta.b: It's open source and runs from GitHub today. Star the repo to follow along.
fq.a.models: Any GGUF model on llama.cpp—Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Gemma, Phi, and thousands more from Hugging Face. See the model catalog for the curated shortlist it recommends from.
fq.q.agent: Can it actually do things, or just chat?
fq.a.agent: It includes a tool-using agent for small tasks, with a hard safety blocklist it cannot override on its own: it will never autonomously pay, delete, transfer money, or touch credentials. Anything sensitive is surfaced for you to confirm.
fq.q.size: How big is a model download?
fq.a.size: Your choice—from about 0.4 GB for the ultra-light build to 9 GB for the flagship. It downloads once over Wi-Fi and resumes in the background if interrupted.
